titleStmt.title: Letter from John Fitzgibbon to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan, circa 1918
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Fitzgibbon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John Fitzgibbon (1845–1919) to Fr. Nolan regarding Jack Fitzgibbon's son, John Fitzgibbon. John Fitzgibbon was a successful draper and MP for South Mayo from 1910-1918. He was also involved in the Gaelic League, the temperance movement and the Department of Agriculture and Technical Instruction (DATI). Father Nolan (1867-1941) was the Provincial of the Irish Province of the Society of Jesus (1912-1922) and was a member of the Distribution Committee which looked after the welfare and distribution of the the Belgian refugees who arrived in Ireland as a consequence of the First World War.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Castlerea My dear Father Nolan, The
enclosed is that
letter to plead
in I am happy
Gods holy work is
done. He was
my joy and pride
and the thought
that when he
Cheerfully gone
to God he would
have made more
use of him on
this earth, Jack
was always full
of energy and
I have not the
slightest doubt
he is now a
busier Man than
ever, we naturally
feel the parting
but are consoled
in the thought of
Larry Shaw an
advocate to secure
for us a speedy
passage to the
Heavenly kingdom.
He loved the order
and I hope I am
not being too presumptuous
in saying that
it's members mourn
his loss and I feel
deeply for them.
With grateful thanks
for your touching
letter I remain
Yours Faithfully John Fitzgibbon
